title: Recurring Task Automation: Reset status column every month/week/x date
excerpt: We have monthly recurring tasks in our SOP boards. I would like the ability to reset the status column every month automatically.

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: We have monthly recurring tasks in our SOP boards. I would like the ability to reset the status column every month automatically.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Melissa

Thanks for the feedback! Can you give some more information on your use case as I think what you’re trying to achieve might already be possible with the automations available?

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to be able to reset a status column in a particular group say on the first of every month or every monday, etc. So if we have Monthly/Weekly tasks on an SOP board, they can reset automatically from Done to Blank.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Melissa!

Would the combination of archiving done tasks and using the automation to create new tasks on a recurring basis work for you? I think it achieves the same thing. Or are you not wanting to lose the updates section when you create new pulses?

posts.author: Krishele (Krishele Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: For us it’d be great if there was an option like Melissa said, to clear statuses but maintain the updates.

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: No, we don’t want things archived. It all needs to stay intact, just the status needs to change just like Krishele mentioned above.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Julia,

I had a very similar discussion with Monday support via email a couple of weeks ago. I was hoping we could set recurring tasks much like recurring tasks in Outlook: set once, set to repeat weekly/monthly. Every week/month, that same task then appears on the board with a reset status and updated due date.

After Monday has passed, for my request, the status gets cleared and the due date gets updated to the next Monday. Becomes a sticky pulse. Not sure if that’s what the others want but definitely want to maintain the updates and info box.

The current automation for creating new tasks creates an item but the due date is static (or blank). Doesn’t get updated each week/month and we lose the updates which won’t work here.

posts.author: Lisa-monday.com (Lisa Ceccato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MStevens and @JohnW

I hear you with the automated process of resetting a pulse status and date on “x” day of the month. I’m going to pass this feedback along to our automations product team!

In the meantime, if looking to reset the status to blank based off the date, this may be of help:

image.png666×594 22.3 KB

Lisa

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: If I use the automation you mention above, how do I specify to reset the status on the first of EVERY month? It seems I have to go in and change the date every month for this to work?

posts.author: JordanD (Jordan Di Conti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Could it work in conjunction with this automation:

So the Automation Lisa provided would change your status from “done” to “blank”. This automation would change the date once the status changes to “blank”. You can push the date 1 week so that every Monday it changes. It’s definitely not ideal but thought it might work until they develop a better option. Here’s what it could be like with the automation Lisa provided listed at the bottom:

image.png907×221 15.9 KB

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: Still need a solution for this one. Unfortunately, the added automation that JordanD suggested above didn’t work. Because our tasks are completed on different days, the deadline dates weren’t consistent. Also, because it was combined with the automation to change the status when deadline arrived and status was x, things were changing more than once and the statuses and deadlines were getting completely off track.

posts.author: lauralev (Laura Levy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi MStevens, gotcha - thanks for the feedback! We’ve taken your request and passed it onto our product team for review. If you have any other ideas, feel free to keep 'em coming!

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: A post was split to a new topic: How to automate vehicle maintenance checks

posts.author: RocketShineWash (Max)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

Has there been any update on this? We have recurring tasks that need to be redone on regular intervals, but I do need their statuses to be clear when we repeat them.

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: Not so far. I had to turn all of mine off and just update manually every week or month as the car may be. It’s extremely frustrating.

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
MStevens:

update manually every week or month

Same here. I cannot find any suitable workaround in Monday at this stage.

posts.author: Gauri (Kristelle (Gauri) Sim)
posts.cookedHtml: I also have submitted this feature request many times through the feature request link, but also to the individual support team members. If Google calendar can do it so well, and seemingly so simply, I wonder what the complication is here? It’s a big problem for us, and I have at least one department that will not use Monday.com because of it.

posts.author: RocketShineWash (Max)
posts.cookedHtml: To be honest, I had assumed they could do this when I signed up. Having tasks repeat without fuss is about a third of the benefit of a PM app for my small company.

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree. As our agency grows won’t be feasible to update all the task manually and we may have to look into a different tool. I spent months getting everything set up in Monday and training the team on how to use it properly and I dread the thought of having to switch us to something else because they can’t do something so simple.

posts.author: JasonMcReynolds (Jason McReynolds)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, the reoccurring tasks are not handled great, even though Monday has other great features. Hope they address this.

posts.author: seanivore (Sean August Horvath)
posts.cookedHtml: you’d think this would be a quick feature to whip up and release due to how sort-of obvious it is that it should already exist. i’m a new user — any experienced user have insight into how long it takes and how often Monday makes these kind of usability updates? pricey tool and looks like this thread started seven months ago. this and having functions be a “Pro” level tool (I literally just want to subtract from a number instead of sum – even their logo for the “numbers” column has more mathematical symbols than can actually be used) are making me question if i made too hasty an investment in this app

posts.author: weblifestores (Fred B)
posts.cookedHtml: I am just going to chime in here and say that not being able to properly manage recurring tasks is a huge disappointment. There are many other things I love about Monday. However, this makes assigning our VA’s repeating tasks very difficult. Even if we do create every task with the 1 automation to create a new task - managing those automatons sucks. There is no way to know what automation you are looking at without clicking through. Please get us a solution Monday!!!

posts.author: bellamyinspires (DARRYL BELLAMY)
posts.cookedHtml: Have you ever switched to a system and thought it was the perfect system for you and THEN realized this SAID SYSTEM, doesn’t allow you to easily do recurring tasks without a pointless automaton? Yup, that’s how I feel. Monday, this really should be standard - please, please look at these multiple threads on multiple sites and listen to your client base. You have something great, don’t mess it up .

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml:
I envision a column called “Timer” so each pulse gets a timer. The options can be a number of days and be displayed in hours, and then the clock starts ticking down. When the timer column reaches 0, the STATUS from X column resets from whatever status is on to whatever you want to rest it to, e.g., if the status is complete by the time the timer reaches 0, it will be reset to status NOT STARTED, that way each pulse gets it’s own “Timer” and each pulse behaves independently from all others.

So you know how you can connect columns, like a date column to a status column, just think of connecting the so-called “Timer” to a status column and when it reaches the 0 the “Timer” changes the status and resets the “Timer” original set time, so if the “Timer” was set at 5 days, it resets itself to 5 days when it reaches 0. This creates a never-ending loop.

I agree with @bellamyinspires that this is a much-needed feature. Just giving my two pennies.

Thanks @lauralev & @Lisa-monday.com !

Just adding a visual.

image1176×417 44.6 KB

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:

its a must-have needed feature.

posts.author: jessicarenewme (Jessica Snow)

posts.author: Thao.SFE (Thao T. )
posts.cookedHtml:
+1. For now, I’m using a status column that I’ve automated to push the “recurring” date by “x amount of days/weeks/months”.

Every time a task is completed, I just push the “snooze/restock” status button that I created.

Hopefully that helps until then.

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for sharing @Thao.SFE! I think other users will find this helpful as a workaround!

posts.author: weblifestores (Fred B)
posts.cookedHtml: Unfortunately if you have tasks with different cadences you need to create a different “snooze status” for each instance. Snooze - 3 days vs Snooze 7 days etc. Any thoughts on this?

posts.author: awesome.dan (Dan)
posts.cookedHtml: Nobody seems to be aware of the Recurring task feature through automation. Please refer to https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000221159

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
@awesome.dan Good to know it now exists.

It did not exist in 2019 and I don’t recall Monday.com announcing it in 2020.

posts.author: MStevens (Melissa Stevens)
posts.cookedHtml: We are all aware of these automations. Unfortunately the recipes available do not fit our use case. I have tried combinations of automations as well and they don’t work properly. I’m hopeful this will be resolved when we are allowed to write our custom automations.

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @MStevens - at this time, the workarounds suggested in this thread are the best ways to reset dates for recurring tasks. Additionally, I have gone ahead and granted your account the ability to make custom automations so you can give these a try!

posts.author: jk193 (Jake K)
posts.cookedHtml:
@melissav-monday.com If I’m understanding correctly, is there no way to create a simple automation that says whenever a pulse is marked done (or archived, that’d work too) on this whole board, automatically duplicate it and set the due date forward by X days or X months? Use case is very common: we have bills that are due each month on the same date e.g. 10th of month, and we want to make sure we remember to pay the vendors on time. When I mark something as done status, or perhaps delete/archive that pulse, somehow another tasks for next month should be created.

Already tried…

  • …the recurring automation to duplicate a group each month, but the due dates don’t get advanced forward
  • …the “every X days, create ___ item”, but this doesn’t seem to allow you to change the date to be relative either, instead requiring you select a fixed calendar date.

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jk193! There are a few different ways you could do this. The ways that you’ve mentioned or the the options below:

  1. Set a custom status label to select when you’ve paid the bill each month (i.e. “Push to next month”), and use the automation below:

image730×536 17.6 KB

  1. Use the following automation to duplicate the item into another group on your board and automatically push the date out as needed:

image720×536 20.2 KB

Do you think either of these options could work for you?

posts.author: jk193 (Jake K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Melissa,

Here’s the solution I came up with:

  1. For each bill, add an automation to create a task each month on a set date (“Every month on the ___, create item ____”)
  2. To handle the reminder column, I have one other automation, “When an item is created, set Reminder Date to date created + 1month”

This way, I can have the tasks created each month at a set time and also get the dynamic reminder/due date col functionality.

Because the automation won’t really start “kicking in” for a month, I just manually created the tasks I need for the first month I’ll be using monday.com

Hope this helps others looking for a way to have recurring reminders without needing to split them into groups!

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @jk193 - that’s great! Thanks so much for sharing with the community

posts.author: jhaydon (Jessica Haydon)
posts.cookedHtml: @melissav-monday.com I too am encountering issues with setting up recurring tasks to function how we would like, and would LOVE the ability to try to customize my own automations. Is there any way you might be able to add this to my account?

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jhaydon - sure no problem!

I do want to mention that the custom automations will still use the same building blocks available with our current automations, you will just have the flexibility to put these blocks together in the way that best fits your workflow.

With that being said, the custom automations might not allow you full customization in order to accomplish what you are looking for with the recurring tasks. Definitely check them out and feel free to write to us at support@monday.com with any specific questions

posts.author: BobWeins (Bob Weins)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. What we need is an automation that allows us to: duplicate a task; adjust the due date (this could be as crude as having it select from a Status or Drop-Down list indicating frequency) and assign it to the same Board and Group. This would also allow us to mark a task as done, duplicate the task (per above) and then move the completed task to another group (if desired). In other words, the automation is useful for recurring items and selected task follow-ups.

posts.author: AYoung (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 here. So it is now Jan 2021 and no progress on this. There are many simple functions that we realise Monday can’t do and we are now committed to 1 year enterprise account let alone the countless hours setting the system up has cost. The whole reason for buying into Monday was to save us money. It is a large overhead with little return at the moment. Monday are very good at sales. Not so good at development.

posts.author: bogdan (Bogdan Chepurny)
posts.cookedHtml: +1000 Please add recurring tasks. Like @BobWeins mentioned, need an automation to duplicate a pulse. Also should reset status, updates, and automatically assign due date with +X days. Such a fundamental function for a PM tool.

posts.author: JamieH.BBI (Jamie)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was disappointed to see that there still hasn’t been a solution in 1.5 years.

This is what I finally figured out after playing with various automations. Unfortunately, I had to make a separate Board for my Monthly Recurring Tasks, Quarterly Recurring Tasks, Bi-Yearly Recurring Tasks, and Yearly Recurring Tasks. Most of these items/tasks are various reports that are done throughout the year. Originally I had Monthly, Quarterly, etc. as different Groups within one Board but quickly realized an automation would not work for one specific group (that’s a separate problem).

For this automation, I’ll use my monthly tasks as an example:
I had to create a Custom Automation and I had to use Date instead of Timeline when setting up my columns. It’s a fairly simple board and doesn’t have other boards linked to it. I’m also not using any additional automations.

"When STATUS changes to DONE push DATE by 29 DAYS and set STATUS to UP NEXT"

I don’t know if someone else had made a similar suggestion, but I figured I’d share what I’ve come up with. By pushing the date automatically and only changing the Status, it keeps all updates and tasks in the same Board without having to archive, duplicate, or manually make multiple changes to EACH task, EACH month. I hope this helps someone else! Good luck.

posts.author: Gauri (Kristelle (Gauri) Sim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, (sigh!), this is a big problem that has gone on for years now, with no real end in sight. Baffling. What I just did for our team is use the one automation that has ‘duplicate item’ in it to generate what we needed:

When Due Date arrives, duplicate item to (Next Month) group and push date by (1 month). I suppose I could make a separate column for different iterations of Due Dates, so that a different column triggers a duplication in the March group, June group, etc. That will make multiple columns that not every item will use, but at least it will get the item in the right month on one board!

Hoping that inspires the Support Team to help us…or if any of you have some better automations, I’d enjoy hearing about them!

posts.author: MikeWolfPAC (Michael Monetta)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday is great at a lot of things and seems to be very responsive to requests, which I appreciate, but this one seems like a glaring blind spot.

All it would it would take, unless I’m missing something, is to add “Change Status” as one of the options under the automation for “Every Time Period.” Right now the only options are “Notify,” “Create Item,” “duplicate group,” and “create group.”

Since nearly everything in Monday revolves around the “Status” column I’m really surprised it’s not one of the options here. This simple addition would solve A TON of issues that are being expressed here. Monthly and weekly automations are crucial and are not the same thing as “adding x days or weeks to a certain date.” Please prioritize this one!!!

posts.author: jdw (John Dantzler-Wolfe)
posts.cookedHtml: Can anyone from Monday speak to this? It’s an essential feature that we are looking to have. We have some tasks that must be completed multiple times per day. So “push the due date by X days” is not a viable workaround. We simply need to be able to say “Update status to X every Y hours” and to keep any notes and updates. Why the lack of transparency from Monday?

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@jdw

I’m not sure if this is the only limitation, but something to keep in mind that’s probably affecting the development of your request. Currently, automations set to go off at certain times aren’t occurring on the dot. Depending on the time of day and how busy the servers are, they can actually be off by a considerable bit. So it would be foolish for Monday to release a feature that operates “every Y hours” if they can’t guarantee a tight time window. I wouldn’t really call it a lack of transparency, rather there is quite a lot of features on the back log still from COVID. Compound that with the likely uptick in Monday usage/users/automations also related to the pandemic, many features that might seem basic actually have long term complicated fixes in the works. The Monday team was just recently able to bring on more members for the Community here, so responses to the most requested features are working their way down the pipeline.

BUT If a feature like you mention is absolutely integral to your workflow, then I would suggest checking out Integromat or Zapier to accomplish it. I’ve just recently set up something similar that checks hourly for meetings and if they occur in the next hour, sends text reminders to the participants. This involves changing statuses and what not, so the feature you’re requesting could easily be accomplished. Integromat is free to try so you might want to start there.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have been testing out various solutions.

Clickup just does this natively with tasks and has a super flexible recurring engine including copying all the related tasks to the next week (we use the task to represent a weekly meeting).

I would have just assumed this is baseline functionality for any software designed for PM and task management.

How can this not be here?

posts.author: patchytoes (Micheal)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team and I used to have recurring task creation in every single solution we have ever tried.
And then we tried Monday dot com…

posts.author: mchinsky (Mark Chinsky)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is the same reason implementing EOS in Monday.com is so hard. You can’t pull a weekly meeting forward each week with prior weeks tasks (or subtasks) intact easily. This was so easy to do in ClickUp! and is a major competitive disadvantage.

Fundamentally, handling to-do’s in Monday is a ‘workaround’ and also forces people into more expensive subscription tiers because you have to use automations to do something that should be native.

posts.author: cadale (Christian)
posts.cookedHtml: They already have “Every Time Period”. Seems like a “Change Status” option should be an easy request.

posts.author: apelkhaz (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump!

Would be great to get an update from Monday on this… Seems like a really sought after feature!

posts.author: user1094587 (JC)
posts.cookedHtml: Just chiming in to reiterate that we could use a feature to reset status or checkmarks every time period (ie. daily).

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

We apologise for the delay in updating this thread, and really appreciate your feedback/comments regarding this feature and our responsiveness to getting back to you all. Just so you are aware, we are working on our feature request process, and sincerely appreciate your patience with us as we get through the backlog

In regard to this particular request, in full transparency, we previously had supported an automation recipe that would clear the specific column value on a recurring basis. Unfortunately we found that it resulted in a number of performance issues, which is why it is currently not available. I want to assure you that our team is aware of the limitation. For the time being, we recommend using the following custom recipe to achieve this set up:

Screen Shot 2022-11-16 at 10.51.52 am1852×682 33.7 KB

Your understanding is greatly appreciated!

posts.author: slkorg (Shawna Organisciak)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! Did this ever get developed or worked around?

posts.author: knewman (Kimberly Newman)
posts.cookedHtml:
For those of you still looking for a workaround on this, I was able to successfully automate this using the below automation:

when status changes to ____, then set status to ____.

You can also use this to have it change due dates for you, so I did:

when status changes to ____, then set status to ____ and then push Due Date by ___.

I’ve found that it is easier to have a Status label that I call “Ready to Reset” to execute this on my end, since I have some items that aren’t recurring and I just want them archived when they’re Done. So create a status label that is for this specific automation, and whenever you change the status to that label, it will push this automation to reset the status and due date automatically for you.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: liftwithdyl (Dylan V)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!! I would really like it just to be automatic feature to reset your tasks, with out having to go in a really round about way of creating “status” “dates” and “resets” etc.

Monday, is there ANYWAY you can just create a feature that claim “When 1st (or any date) of the wee/month/year arrives, reset column value to empty” or something?? It’s so confusing and extremely time consuming to have to figure out how to scrape it all together! Thank you x

posts.author: bade (Mikada)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
Recurring Task Automations App includes 4 different recipes that update or clear columns recurringly
Every time period set status to something for this item

Every time period assing person as assignee for this item

Every time period clear column for this item

Every time period set number to value for this item
